subcodes.title: Words to some friends, spoken on 1914-12-03
subcodes.first_line: Man must possess the religion of God which is the source of all valour, moral and intellectual. If he has not this, he may have at least, the simple 'natural religion' of humanity.
subcodes.subjects: @courage, @religion as reality; definitions of religion, @religion as basis of morality; of divine civilization, @tests and trials; sacrifice and suffering
